t_ch: 这只是…第一拨敌人。它们…很快就会再集结。我不能……
t_en: This is... only the first wave. They'll have reassembled soon... I have to...

t_ch: 听你姐姐的话吧，铁卫！一看就知道，你的状态还没完全恢复呢。
t_en: Hey, Mr. Silvermane, listen to your sister! I can tell just by looking at you that you haven't fully recovered.

t_ch: 外来的、希儿——趁着下一波攻势还没开始，你们赶紧突围吧！
t_en: Outsiders, Seele, the second wave hasn't started yet. Make the most of this opportunity and make a break for it!

t_ch: 欸？希露瓦，你不和我们一起去找「星核」吗？
t_en: Huh? Serval, aren't you gonna look for the Stellaron with us?

t_ch: 老弟独木难支啊。刚才把他教训了一顿，这是我的责任。而且……
t_en: My brother can't hold them back by himself. He wasted a lot of energy on us. He's my responsibility now. Besides...

t_ch: 我不帮把手的话…下一场战斗不知道会怎么样呢。
t_en: If I'm not here to help... I don't know what might happen in the next wave.

t_ch: 我也很想跟你们一起啊，真的很想！「星核」近在咫尺了，离我那么近，我一直想看它一眼——
t_en: I want to go with you, I do! I always wanted to catch a glimpse of the Stellaron with my own eyes, and now it's so close...

t_ch: 但总不能丢下老弟和这群铁卫不管吧，我可是朗道家的女儿啊！交给你们了！我相信你们能做到，我在你们身上看到了希望！
t_en: But I can't abandon my brother and the other Guards — I'm a Landau! It's up to you now, I believe in you. You've given me new hope!

t_ch: 赶在可可利亚发觉之前，把「星核」拿到手吧！
t_en: Get hold of the Stellaron before Cocolia realizes what's happening!

t_ch: 我也不能丢下你们不管啊！
t_en: We can't abandon you either!

t_ch: 希露瓦说的对，{NICKNAME}。我们走吧。
t_en: Serval's right, {NICKNAME}. Let's go.

t_ch: 铁卫为我们打开了通路，不要辜负他们。
t_en: The Guards have opened up a way through for us, we can't let them down.

t_ch: 我们不会辜负你的信任。
t_en: We won't disappoint you.

t_ch: 噢噢噢噢噢噢噢噢噢！
t_en: Chaaaaarge!

t_ch: 「星核」的模样…记得拍张照给我啊，小姑娘。
t_en: Hey March! Don't forget to take a picture of the Stellaron for me.

t_ch: 一言为定！放心吧，我们可是很厉害的！
t_en: Say no more! We're good at this. Don't worry!

t_ch: 所以呢？你们把那批有问题的机器人丢在哪儿了？
t_en: So? What did you do with those defective robots?

t_ch: 呃…那个……
t_en: Ahem... well...

t_ch: 麻利点！干嘛扭扭捏捏的？
t_en: Come on! What are you squirming around for?

t_ch: 负责军备管理的同事，忘了把残次品的事同步给指挥官了……
t_en: The worker in charge of arms management forgot to report the defective units to the commanding officer...

t_ch: 那批机器人，可能已经被部署到禁区里了。
t_en: Those robots may have already been deployed to the restricted zone.

t_ch: 你说啥！？
t_en: WHAT!?

t_ch: 别、别生气嘛！我已经把这次事故整理成报告，向上级提交了…现在只等上头派人处理这事就行！
t_en: Don't get angry! I have written a report of this incident and submitted it to management...We just need to wait for them to send someone to deal with it!

t_ch: 不过你也知道我们的审批流程是个什么状况…在那之前咱也只能坐着干等着了。
t_en: But you know what our approval system is like... Until then, we can't really do anything.

t_ch: 干等着？这件事如果不及时处理，迟早要闹出人命！这种状况下你难道坐得住吗？！
t_en: We can't just wait here and do nothing! There will be big trouble if we don't deal with this now! How can you just sit around so calmly!?

t_ch: 这…话是在理，可咱就只是个文职，根本对付不了发疯的机器人啊……
t_en: You are right... But we are just small potatoes. There is no way we can deal with those crazy robots...

t_ch: 唉，气死我了…戍卫队的人呢？是他们没把好质量这关，让这批垃圾流进禁区的！这帮人总该出面解释一下吧？
t_en: Argh! This makes me so angry... Where are the Garrison people? They are to blame for the restricted zone receiving this batch of subpar garbage since they didn't do enough quality control! Shouldn't those people come forward and provide us an explanation?

t_ch: 他们…我联系过了，不管我怎么说，他们都只说是技术部验收结果不牢靠，和他们没半点关系。意思是让我们自己想办法处理……
t_en: Well... I've contacted them but no matter what I say, they just blame it on the Technology Division, saying that they're unreliable and the Garrison had nothing to do with it. Which means we have to deal with it ourselves...

t_ch: …混账！这都什么时候了，还在这儿踢皮球？
t_en: That's ridiculous! How can they shirk their responsibility like that?

t_ch: 需要搭把手吗？
t_en: Do you need a hand?

t_ch: （不理会他们的争吵）
t_en: (Ignore their quarrel)

t_ch: 哈？你哪个部门的？
t_en: Huh? Which department are you from?

t_ch: 我们在城里见过面…
t_en: We met in the city before...

t_ch: 是吗？完全没印象了。
t_en: We did? I don't remember you at all.

t_ch: 热心肠的路人。
t_en: A kindhearted passerby.

t_ch: 哼，这地方还有乐于助人的家伙？我可不信。
t_en: Humph! A kindhearted passerby in this place? I doubt it.

t_ch: 等等，你们…莫非是通缉令上的「外来者」？之前筑城者兴师动众想要抓住的人…难道就是你们？
t_en: Wait! You are... aren't you the "Outsider" on the warrant that the Architects were so desperate to apprehend not so long ago?

t_ch: 可这里是军事管制区啊，按说连只雪地鼠都进不来…你是怎么出现在这里的？
t_en: But this is a military restricted zone, and not even a snow mouse is supposed to be able to enter... How did you manage to get in?

t_ch: 哎呀算了算了，不管了！你要是能跟铁卫对着干，对付几个发疯的机器人应该不算什么吧？
t_en: Oh, whatever! If you can put up a fight against the Silvermane Guards, those crazy robots must just be a piece of cake for you, right?

t_ch: 听着，我这儿有事需要你处理，非常紧急！这可是人命关天的大事！
t_en: Listen! I have something very urgent that I need your help with. It's a matter of life and death!

t_ch: 你就这么信任我？
t_en: You trust me?

t_ch: 我还有什么办法？看看这帮家伙，有一个算一个都是废物！
t_en: What else can I do? Look at these guys. All of them are useless scrubs!

t_ch: 这么说未免太过分了……
t_en: That's a bit harsh...

t_ch: 救人性命是我的天职。
t_en: Saving people is my passion.

t_ch: 嘴上说说谁不会啊？希望你真的靠得住。
t_en: Easier said than done! I hope you're really that reliable.

t_ch: 我给你梳理一下——戍卫队上个月订制了一批警卫机器人，因为制造班的产能过于紧张，所以把一部分工作外包给了城里的私人制造厂……
t_en: Let me give you a rundown first... So, last month, the Garrison ordered a batch of guardbots. And at the time, the manufacturing team was so overstretched that there was no other option but to outsource some workloads to a private manufacturer in the city.

t_ch: 谁知道这批机器人的质量完全没达到验收标准，有严重的失控隐患！
t_en: And it turns out that this batch of robots is simply subpar, and there is a real danger that they could go out of control.

t_ch: 后面的事估计你也听到了，我就不说了。总之，我希望你能尽快找到那些失控的机器人，回收他们的<color=#dbc291ff>「核心模组」</color>！
t_en: I guess you've already heard what happened afterwards, so I'll say no more. In short, I hope you can deal with those out of control robots as soon as possible and take back their <color=#dbc291ff>"Core Modules"</color>!

t_ch: 我才不管你们用什么手段。电磁干扰，遥控，或者干脆把它们砸得稀巴烂——反正都是一堆劣质的玩意……
t_en: I don't care how you do it... Electromagnetic interference, remote control, or simply smashing them to pieces! They are useless anyway...

t_ch: 失控机器的出现位置我已经大致标记好了。赶紧去吧，没时间磨蹭了！
t_en: I've marked the general location of those robots. Hurry up! We don't have much time left!

t_ch: 问题机器人现在在哪儿？
t_en: Where are the faulty robots now?

t_ch: 呃、那个…先别急……
t_en: Well... Um... relax...

t_ch: ——这机器人是有什么毛病？！它识别不出我们是自己人吗？
t_en: What's wrong with this robot? It doesn't recognize us?

t_ch: 不知道啊，这台好像是戍卫队那边新投放的机器人…咱俩就这么把它拆了，队长不会找咱们麻烦吧？
t_en: I have no idea! It seems to be a new robot from the Garrison... If we dismantle it like this without telling the Captain, will we get into any trouble?

t_ch: 这批机器人失控了，我来回收。
t_en: It's a faulty robot. Let me handle it.

t_ch: 啊？这批机器人不是新货吗，怎么突然就…是不是戍卫队没仔细验收啊？
t_en: Uh, aren't they brand-new? How come... Is it because the Garrison didn't do proper quality control?

t_ch: …算了算了，别说了，这不是咱该追究的。
t_en: Just leave it be... It's not our job to pursue this matter.

t_ch: 这是你们干的？
t_en: You did this?

t_ch: 是啊！我俩正站在这儿好端端地聊天呢，这机器人就突然冲过来了！
t_en: Yeah! We were standing here chatting and the robot suddenly charged over!

t_ch: 情急之下只好当场进行无害化处理了…以前从没发生过这样的事，吓我一跳。
t_en: The only thing I could do at the time was to dismantle it... I've never had a robot charge me like that before. It was quite the fright.

t_ch: 你是来回收它的？我们已经把「核心模组」拆下来了，你们好好研究一下哪儿出问题了吧。以后可别再出现这种事故了，怪吓人的……
t_en: Are you here to retrieve it? We have already removed the Core Module. Please look into this matter and don't let this happen again! It was so scary...

t_ch: 花点时间好好想想——这次外包项目的验收流程具体都有谁参与？把你知道的名字都告诉我。
t_en: We should take a moment to think this through — who is involved in the quality control process for this outsourcing project? Tell me all the names you can think of.

t_ch: 谢、谢尔盖妮娅长官，我只是个经办的小文职啊，知道的真没您想象的那么多……
t_en: M—Miss Sergeynia, I am just a researcher here, I don't know as much as you think...

t_ch: …唉。第一，我的姓氏是谢尔盖耶夫娜；第二，没必要对我阿谀奉承，叫我佩拉就好。加不加长官随你便，我不在乎……
t_en: Sigh... Firstly, my last name is Sergeyevna. Secondly, you don't have to patronize me. Just call me Pela. I don't care if you call me "Officer" or not.

t_ch: ——我只在乎能不能以高效的方式妥当处置这次事故，而这取决于你能不能再从脑袋里挖出更有用的信息。不要浪费时间，这对你我都有好处。
t_en: I only care about handling this incident properly and efficiently, and that depends on whether you can give me useful information. Don't waste our time!

t_ch: 咿…！我想，我想……
t_en: Eh... I think... Um...

t_ch: 呃…这里发生什么事了？
t_en: Oh...What happened here?

t_ch: 啊…你、你来了！那个，你可以先等一下……
t_en: Oh... You are here! Well, just wait a moment...

t_ch: 看来你还对我瞒了不少事啊…比如这位突然出现在军事禁区的{M#先生}{F#小姐}——
t_en: Looks like you're keeping quite a lot of things from me... like this {M#gentleman}{F#lady} here who suddenly appeared in the military restricted zone...

t_ch: 我都搞定了，茉莉人呢？
t_en: I'm all done! Where is Molly?

t_ch: 啊…你、你来了！那个，茉莉她……
t_en: Oh... You are here! Well... Molly...

t_ch: 看来你还对我瞒了不少事啊…比如这位突然出现在军事禁区的{M#先生}{F#小姐}，还有那位茉莉——
t_en: Looks like you're keeping quite a lot of things from me... like this {M#gentleman}{F#lady} here who suddenly appeared in the military restricted zone... also that Molly...

t_ch: 佩～拉～姐～姐～
t_en: Missss~ Pelaaaa~!

t_ch: …请个别大人不要——
t_en: Please don't call my name like that...

t_ch: 呃，怎么是你？不对……
t_en: Why are you here? This is strange...

t_ch: ——怎么又是你？
t_en: How come it's you again...?

t_ch: 杰帕德是叫我不要为难「外来者」…但这铁卫禁区可不是让你们四处闲晃的地方啊。
t_en: Gepard did tell me not to make things difficult for the "outsider"... but the Silvermane Guard Restricted Zone isn't just somewhere I can let you hang around.

t_ch: 我来帮忙解决次品机器人事件。
t_en: I am here to deal with those defective robots.

t_ch: 那你和这次事件是脱不了干系了——坦白从宽、抗拒从严，老实交代吧！
t_en: Then this time you can't just say it's none of your business... Be honest and tell me the truth!

t_ch: 我只是做了热心市民该做的事…
t_en: I just did what a good citizen would do...

t_ch: …你知道吗？热心市民擅闯军事禁区可是犯法的。
t_en: Hey... But you do know that it's still illegal for a good citizen to enter the military restricted zone, right?

t_ch: 不过，既然你出现在这里了，那肯定和这次事件脱不了干系——坦白从宽、抗拒从严，老实交代吧！
t_en: However, since you are here... you must have something to do with this. Be honest and tell me the truth!

t_ch: 在佩拉的盘问下，你坦白了自己了解到的全部内容……
t_en: Under Pela's interrogation, you confessed everything...

t_ch: 嗯…情况我都了解了，和我推理的结果基本一致——既然那位茉莉小姐和你都参与到了这次事件里，那一切都说得通了。
t_en: Ah... I understand now. It is more or less as I surmised... Since Miss Molly and you are involved in this incident, then everything makes sense now.

t_ch: 至于那些<color=#dbc291ff>「核心模组」</color>，你就先交给我吧。
t_en: As to those <color=#dbc291ff>"Core Modules"</color>... hand them to me first.

t_ch: 好了，研究员先生…对此您想发表什么评论吗？你的口供好像和这位{M#先生}{F#小姐}的有点出入嘛。
t_en: Alright, Mr. Researcher... Do you want to comment on this? Your statement seems to be a little bit different from this {M#gentleman}{F#lady}'s.

t_ch: 我、我只是怕长官您查到茉莉头上…虽然她是性子急了点，可这次事故和她没半分关系哪…我、我保证！饶了我吧……
t_en: Well... I was just afraid you'd make Molly a suspect. She can be a bit impatient at times, but I assure you that this incident has nothing to do with her! Please just spare me, Captain...

t_ch: 放心，你和那位茉莉小姐都不会有事的。只是以后再遇到这种事，别兜圈子——还记得我说过的吗？不要浪费时间，这对你我都有好处。
t_en: Don't worry, you and Molly will be fine. Just don't play silly games with me when it happens again in the future. Remember what I said? Don't waste my time!

t_ch: 谢、谢谢您，谢尔盖科夫娅长官！
t_en: Oh... Thank you... thank you so much Captain Sergekevna!

t_ch: 其实是佩拉格娅•谢尔盖…
t_en: Her name is Pelageya Sergey...

t_ch: …算了。我习惯了，就这样吧。
t_en: ...Well, whatever. I've gotten used to it.

t_ch: 那接下来你要怎么做？
t_en: So what are you going to do next?

t_ch: 虽然我很想追究你擅闯军事禁区这件事——但看在希露瓦对你能力和品行高度评价的份上，我想拜托你协助我处理这起事故…就当是将功补过吧。
t_en: Even though I really want to hold you responsible for your trespassing into the military zone, I will however let it slide this time, since Serval vouched for your ability and character. I would also like to ask you to help me deal with the incident, which is a chance for you to make up for your mistakes.

t_ch: 长话短说——这起机器人事故只是我在追查的事件的其中一环。这里面的利益纠葛非常复杂，而那位茉莉小姐很可能已经触动了某些人的神经……
t_en: Long story short, this robot incident is just one chapter in my ongoing investigation. The stakes here are very high, and Miss Molly may have ruffled some feathers.

t_ch: 总之，她现在很可能有危险。
t_en: So... she might be in danger now...

t_ch: 我再问你最后一个问题：你知道茉莉去哪儿了吗？
t_en: I am going to ask you one last question: Do you know where Molly is?

t_ch: 她？她家好像出了点事…就先跑回去查看情况了。刚才有个戍卫队的铁卫来找她单独谈话，具体谈了什么内容我是没听到，但她的脸色立刻变得很难看……
t_en: Well... There seemed to be a family emergency, so she ran back to check on them. It was a Silvermane Guard from the Garrison who came to send her the message. I don't know what they talked about, but she looked dreadful afterwards.

t_ch: 我觉得…你们可以去行政区，废弃商业街入口旁有栋公寓。茉莉好像就住在那里……
t_en: I think... you can go to the Administrative District. There is an apartment building next to the abandoned business street. I think Molly is there.

t_ch: …看来我预想的没错，她碰上麻烦了。
t_en: ...I guess I was right. She's in trouble.

t_ch: 我先走一步，请你尽快跟上来。
t_en: I'll go first. Please follow me as soon as possible.

t_ch: ——我就知道是你们干的！你们有没有想过，要是因为这批残次品闹出了人命怎么办？
t_en: ...I knew it was you guys who did it! Have you all ever thought about what happens if people die from those defective robots?

t_ch: 咋办？哼，我告诉你咋办——就算真出了什么事，黑锅也是你们技术部来背。
t_en: What happens? Hmph, I'll tell you... even if something really happens, your Technology Division will take the blame!
You...

t_ch: 茉莉啊茉莉…我警告过你很多次了，禁区里的事，你只要睁一只眼闭一只眼就行了——只要你不蹚浑水，说不定我们还能分你一杯羹……
t_en: Come on, Molly! I have warned you many times to keep your mouth shut about the things in the restricted zone. You know how it goes, you scratch our backs and we will scratch yours...

t_ch: 放弃吧，小姑娘！你以为只靠自己就能粉碎贝洛伯格所有军商串谋的勾当？这水可比你想的深多了。
t_en: Give it up, little girl... You think you alone can put an end to all of Belobog's shady dealings between its military and its merchants? This is so much bigger than you could possibly imagine.

t_ch: 我们最多也就是抄了你的家，要是惹到了上面的人…那后果你根本不敢想！
t_en: All we've done so far is just a little "do-over" of your house, but if you ever provoke the ire of those higher-ups... You won't even dare to think about the consequences!

t_ch: 住嘴，败类！
t_en: Shut up, scum!

t_ch: 有话好好说，别动手嘛。
t_en: Calm down...

t_ch: 给我谢尔盖耶夫娜个面子…
t_en: For the sake of Miss Sergeyevna...

t_ch: 你…唉，算了……
t_en: You... *sigh*... Never mind...

t_ch: 够了！都给我住手！你们，把手里的武器放在地上，双手抱头！动作快！
t_en: That's enough! Put your weapons down and put your hands up! Quick!

t_ch: 这、这又是什么情况？！
t_en: Wh... what happened!?

t_ch: 啊，我知道了…行啊丫头，长本事了，还知道搬救兵了？
t_en: Oh... I get it! Good one, little girl... you found someone to help, did you?

t_ch: 哼，可惜只是来了两个弱不禁风的家伙！我告诉你们，今天就是戍卫官本人来了，也没他的好果子吃！
t_en: Hmph... Unfortunately, they are only two weaklings! Let me tell you, even if the captain himself were here, I wouldn't be scared off!

t_ch: 口气真大啊，看来你们是没把我这情报官放在眼里…这可是个大问题，之后也要提上日程了。
t_en: Wow... It seems you aren't taking me seriously at all. I am an intelligence officer! This is a major case, and I just might have to put it on my agenda!

t_ch: 我们上，{NICKNAME}。下手再重也没关系——铁卫的军医技术可是一流的。
t_en: Let's kick their butt, {NICKNAME}! No need to hold back! As you know, the Silvermane Guards have first-class medical support.

t_ch: 嗯？茉莉怎么跑来了，还先了我们一步？
t_en: Huh? Why is Molly here? And she got here before us?

t_ch: …看来她遇到麻烦了。快，我们上去看看情况！
t_en: ...Looks like she's in trouble. Quick! Let's check it out!

t_ch: 在佩拉的指挥下，犯罪分子被赶到现场的铁卫们押送至禁闭室，等待进一步处置……
t_en: The Silvermane Guards rushed to the scene, and under Pela's command, they led the criminals to the confinement room, where they'll be awaiting their punishment...

t_ch: …已经结束了吗？
t_en: ...Is it over?

t_ch: 你安全了。
t_en: You are safe now.

t_ch: 我还以为他们真的是硬茬…
t_en: I thought they'd be tougher....

t_ch: 你怎么还有心情开玩笑啊……
t_en: I wish you wouldn't make light of the matter.

t_ch: 看刚才他们那架势，如果没有你们及时出手的话，我恐怕已经……
t_en: They were not bluffing, you know. If it weren't for you guys helping me out in time, I'm afraid I would have been...

t_ch: 我、我不知道该说什么好…太谢谢你们了！
t_en: I... um... I don't know what to say... Thank you so much!

t_ch: 你没受伤就好。这群人私通黑商的物证我已经找到了…你应该也有要找的东西吧，茉莉小姐？
t_en: Thank goodness you weren't hurt. Also, I've found hard evidence of all their illicit dealings with those shady merchants. I believe you were looking for something as well. Is that right, Miss Molly?

t_ch: 嗯，已经找到了！而且完好无损，真是万幸……
t_en: Indeed, and I found it! And luckily it's still in good condition...

t_ch: 没事就好。
t_en: Good to hear!

t_ch: 这是什么？
t_en: What's this?

t_ch: 啊…这是从我祖上一路传下来的宝贝。虽然我也不知道它里面装的到底是什么…但它对我来说十分重要。
t_en: Oh... This is a family heirloom. Even though I don't know exactly what's inside, it's still very important to me.

t_ch: ——还有一件很严肃的事要问你，茉莉小姐。你怎么会先于我们出现在这里？理性而论，你此时最该出现的地方应该是机械屋才对。
t_en: Miss Molly, I do have another very serious question to ask you... How did you manage to show up before we did? If anything, you should be at the workshop now.

t_ch: 我、我只是……
t_en: Well... I...

t_ch: …我只是觉得，这件事毕竟因我而起，所以也应该由我……
t_en: I was thinking about how all this only happened because of me, so... I thought I should...

t_ch: …这可不是什么好理由。茉莉小姐，虽然我不想把话说得太难听，但对于你这次的行为，我只能评价为有勇无谋。
t_en: That is not a good excuse, Miss Molly. No offense, but you were simply too reckless.

t_ch: 我欣赏你的勇气和责任感…但你缺少对当前状况作出准确评估的能力，还经常把自己置于险境。
t_en: I do admire your bravery and sense of duty... but you're still lacking in situational judgment and thus often end up putting yourself in harm's way.

t_ch: 你有想象过自己该如何独自对抗那些恶棍吗？退一步说，就算你真打倒了那群喽啰…你有对抗他们幕后黑手的手段吗？
t_en: Have you really given much thought to how you could win the fight against those thugs all on your own? Let's be charitable here and assume you could beat them. What then? Are you really equipped to take on the criminal mastermind behind the scenes?

t_ch: …唉，佩拉长官教训的是。这次是我没有考虑清楚后果就擅自行动了…而且那些家伙说的也没错，只凭我一个人的信念，什么都改变不了。
t_en: *sigh* You are right, Officer Pela. I didn't think carefully enough about the consequences of my action. And those goons weren't entirely off the mark when they said I couldn't take on the whole world just on my own.

t_ch: 说什么让贝洛伯格变得更好…不过是痴人说梦而已。我连自己的人生都把握不住……
t_en: I thought I could make Belobog a better place... Well, I can't even manage my own life...

t_ch: ——我拼死拼活努力到现在，到底有什么意义…？
t_en: I've been working so hard... but what's the point?

t_ch: 确实毫无意——
t_en: It's indeed all for naught.

t_ch: …喂，打住！你别说话了。
t_en: Hey! Cut it out.

t_ch: 你改变不了所有人。
t_en: You can't change everyone.

t_ch: 难道我的梦想真的是个笑话吗？什么公正平等的城市，在这里压根就不可能实现……
t_en: Is my dream really a joke? A city that stands for justice and equality... it's impossible in this world!

t_ch: 这…茉莉，不要这样…刚才是我言重了！我不是想打击你……
t_en: Well... Molly, please don't get nihilistic... Maybe I was being a bit too harsh just now. I didn't mean to upset you...

t_ch: …对不起，我没事，只是突然有些控制不住情绪。我还是听佩拉士官的话，先回机械屋吧……
t_en: ...Sorry, I'm fine. I just couldn't help myself. I'd better listen to Officer Pela and head back to the workshop...

t_ch: …或许这份工作真的不适合我？唉……
t_en: Perhaps this job is not for me after all. *sigh*...

t_ch: 惹到那些权势滔天的家伙…现在军队里恐怕已经没有我的位置了吧？唉……
t_en: I've messed with the wrong people. I'm afraid I don't have a place in the military anymore... *sigh*...

t_ch: 我…究竟该怎么办才好…？
t_en: What should I do...?

t_ch: 你先带着她回机械屋吧，我还得去处理这次事故的后续事宜……
t_en: Take her back to the workshop. I have to follow up on this incident...

t_ch: 要是你有空的话，我们晚点在机械屋见吧。茉莉看起来需要和人多聊聊天……
t_en: Let's meet at the workshop later if you are free. Looks like Molly needs someone to talk to...

t_ch: 这里到处都是铁锈和烟尘的味道…我不太喜欢。
t_en: It reeks of rust and soot here... I don't like it one bit.

t_ch: 但斯捷潘先生说过，这也是我应尽的责任。只有照他说的做，我才能赢得大家的尊重，过上好的生活。
t_en: But Mr. Stefan said that this is my responsibility too. If I just do as he says, I'll earn everyone's respect and be able to live a good life.

t_ch: 可我该从哪里开始？我在追求的到底是什么？什么都想不起来，什么都…我需要他的帮助……
t_en: But where should I start? What is it that I'm looking for? I can't think of anything... nothing... I need his help...

t_ch: …希莉儿？
t_en: ...Cyrille?

t_ch: 你…长大了？
t_en: Have you... grown up?

t_ch: 欸？你是…我们之前见过面吗？
t_en: Hmm? Are you... have we met before?

t_ch: 抱歉，我…我现在的状态不太好。很多事记不起来，很多事想不明白……
t_en: Sorry, I'm... my head is not in a good place. I can't remember much of anything at all, and I'm not able to think a lot...

t_ch: 你又被箱子给「困住」了吗？
t_en: Have you been "trapped" by your box again?

t_ch: 「困住」？唔…我现在有点混乱，没法完全理解……
t_en: "Trapped"? Hmm... hah, I'm quite confused right now, and I can't really understand...

t_ch: 你是怎么离开边缘通路的？
t_en: How did you leave Backwater Pass?

t_ch: 离开？离开哪里？我不记得自己从哪来，也不记得自己离开过任何地方……
t_en: Leave? Leave where? I can't remember where I'm from, and I can't remember leaving any place...

t_ch: 我记得你…比现在年轻很多。
t_en: I remember you... back when you were much younger.

t_ch: 但是…我对你完全没有印象了，对不起——我现在有点混乱……
t_en: But... I don't have any recollection of you whatsoever. I'm sorry — I'm a little confused at the moment...

t_ch: 斯捷潘先生说过，我不需要靠自己做出艰难的选择。只要是非常重要的场合…他一定会在我身边。所以…我只要等待就好了。
t_en: Mr. Stefan said that I don't have to make hard choices on my own. If it's an important enough occasion... then he'll be there for me. So... I'll just wait.

t_ch: （虽然外表是成年人…但她的思绪好像还是和上次一样混乱。）
t_en: (She may appear to be an adult... but her mind seems to be just as muddled as before.)

t_ch: （她一直在等的那个人，是叫斯捷潘吗？他好像一直没有出现。或许我可以再试着帮帮她……）
t_en: (Is the person she's been waiting for this whole time called Stefan? It doesn't look like he's shown up yet. Perhaps I can try to help her again...)

t_ch: 解决这个箱子谜题能帮到你吗？
t_en: Will solving this box puzzle help you?

t_ch: 我…不知道。
t_en: I... don't know.

t_ch: 我想试着帮你理清思绪。
t_en: I want to try and help you clear your mind.

t_ch: …真的吗？你有办法？
t_en: ...Really? Do you know how?

t_ch: 谢谢你。我…不常接受陌生人的帮助，所以不知道该怎么表达。但我真的很感谢你。
t_en: Thank you. I... don't usually accept help from strangers, so I don't really know how to show my thanks. But I really am grateful to you.

t_ch: 这些箱子…我不知道它们和我有什么关联。但我没法把视线从它们身上移开…也读不出它们的意义。
t_en: These boxes... I don't know what connection they have with me, but I can't take my eyes off them... and I can't figure out what they mean.

t_ch: 也许解开了这个谜…不，不能期望太多。斯捷潘先生说过，要抛弃不切实际的奢望…一切必须由我们自己争取。
t_en: Maybe by solving this puzzle... no, I can't get my hopes up. Mr. Stefan said to forget about any unrealistic expectations... Everything must be fought and won by ourselves.

t_ch: ——我就在这里等你，哪儿也不去。
t_en: I'll wait for you here. I'm not going anywhere.

t_ch: …你怎么了？
t_en: ...What's wrong?

t_ch: …有什么变化吗？
t_en: ...Has anything changed?

t_ch: 我…好像记起了些东西。
t_en: I... think I remember something.

t_ch: 我看到了…人，很多很多人。他们聚集在一片空地上，一起抬头望着某样东西……
t_en: I saw... people, lots and lots of people. They're gathered in a clearing, and they're all looking up at something...

t_ch: 是我。他们在看…我。
t_en: It's me. They're looking at... me.

t_ch: 斯捷潘先生在那儿，就在我身边。姐姐没来…我真希望她也在。
t_en: Mr. Stefan is there, and he's with me. My sister's not there... I really wish she were.

t_ch: （保持沉默，继续聆听。）
t_en: (Stay silent and keep listening.)

t_ch: 什么时候的事？发生在哪？
t_en: When did this happen? Where did it happen?

t_ch: 我…想不起来。
t_en: I... can't remember.

t_ch: 等等…我好像…还听到了一些声音，是从人群中发出的声音。
t_en: Wait... I think I can... hear a voice, a voice coming out of the crowd.

t_ch: 他们…他们在说一些不好的话。诉苦、抱怨，还有…咒骂。
t_en: They... they're saying bad things. Complaining, grumbling, and... cursing.

t_ch: 那些话…好像是在对我说的。他们讨厌我，蔑视我，甚至…憎恨我。我不明白……
t_en: Those bad words... seem to be aimed at me. They don't like me. They despise me, and they even... hate me. I don't understand...

t_ch: 但是斯捷潘先生握住了我的手。他在微笑，他对我从来都是微笑…他说一切都会好起来，没什么好担心的。
t_en: But Mr. Stefan is holding my hand. He's smiling, and he's always smiling at me... He says everything will get better and there's nothing to worry about.

t_ch: 我相信他，因为他从来没对我撒过谎。那些人的话…不重要。我只要在意他的声音就行了。
t_en: I trust him because he's never lied to me before. Those bad words... don't matter. All I care about is his voice.

t_ch: 然后…然后……
t_en: And then... and then...

t_ch: （保持沉默，继续聆听。）
t_en: (Stay silent and keep listening.)

t_ch: 然后？
t_en: Then?

t_ch: 然后…是黑暗。我只能记起这些片段…除此以外是一片黑暗。
t_en: And then... darkness. All I can remember are these flashbacks... other than that, it's total darkness.

t_ch: 但至少我找回了些东西——这是不是能证明…你刚才做的事是有效果的？
t_en: But at least I've got something back — does this prove that... what you just did actually worked?

t_ch: 你现在觉得自由了吗？
t_en: Do you feel free now?

t_ch: 不。虽然记忆恢复了一部分…但我还是哪儿也去不了。
t_en: No. Even though I've got some of my memories back... I still can't go anywhere.

t_ch: 还要再来几次吗？
t_en: Should I do it a few more times?

t_ch: 我感觉到了，困住我的力量…又在逐渐接近我。一步一步…又要重新把我裹入黑暗……
t_en: I can feel the power trapping me... coming nearer again. It's coming, one step at a time... to wrap me in darkness once again...

t_ch: 你眨了一下眼，然后……
t_en: You blink, and then...

t_ch: （一眨眼的功夫，那些箱子就又出现了——和在边缘通路遇到的情况几乎一模一样。）
t_en: (In the blink of an eye, the boxes reappear — just like how they did at Backwater Pass.)

t_ch: 我打不破这个循环。我不知道该怎么努力……
t_en: I can't break this cycle. I don't know what to do...

t_ch: 我感受不到过去，也看不见未来。我好像在一片雪地里独自漂泊，四周都是一模一样的黑夜。
t_en: I can't feel the past, and I can't see the future. I seem to be drifting alone in a blizzard, surrounded by nothing but darkness.

t_ch: 但我还是想找回属于自己的记忆，哪怕只是零星的片段。至少…捕获那些碎片以后，我能获得一丝安心感。
t_en: But I still want to get my own memories back, even if it's just a few tiny pieces. At least... I feel a little bit better having recovered those fragments.

t_ch: 我会尽力帮你找回自我。
t_en: I'll try my best to help you regain yourself.

t_ch: 我比较悲观…但我还是会帮你。
t_en: I doubt if it will work... but I'll still help you.

t_ch: 谢谢你，陌生人。
t_en: Thank you, stranger.

t_ch: 我现在也能安心仰仗其他人了…如果让斯捷潘先生知道了这件事，他会开心吗？
t_en: I can place my trust in people now... Would Mr. Stefan be happy if he knew about this?

t_ch: …记忆恢复了吗？
t_en: ...Is your memory back yet?

t_ch: …这次有什么其它反应吗？
t_en: ...Any other side-effects this time?

t_ch: （只是沉默地等着。）
t_en: (Just wait in silence.)

t_ch: 记忆…终于…我终于记起来了。我的身份，我的过去，我在寻找的东西……
t_en: I remember... finally... I can remember. My identity, my past, what I'm looking for...

t_ch: …全都卷成了一团，我得费上很多力气才能把它们捋清楚，然后再一根一根地抽出来。我想我能做到…靠自己的力量。
t_en: ...It's shrunk up into a ball. It's hard to untangle it all, so I have to pull out each memory, one by one. I think I can do it... all on my own.

t_ch: 所以，你究竟是谁？
t_en: So who exactly are you?

t_ch: 我是……
t_en: I am...

t_ch: 对不起…对不起，我不该在这种情况下曝露自己！我知道你没有恶意，我愿意信任你——但斯捷潘先生说过…他说过……
t_en: Sorry... I'm sorry. I shouldn't risk my privacy like this! I know you mean no harm, and I do trust you — but Mr. Stefan said... he said...

t_ch: 对不起，我不能…如果可以的话，请你把希莉儿这个名字也从记忆里抹去吧！就假装…假装我们从来都没见过面。
t_en: I'm sorry. I just can't... If you can, please remove the name Cyrille from your memory, too! Just pretend... pretend that we've never even met.

t_ch: 你在寻找什么？
t_en: What is it that you're looking for?

t_ch: 我…因为一件事……
t_en: I... it's just this thing...

t_ch: 我辜负了斯捷潘先生的信任。他那么相信我，但我却…连这么简单的事都没做好。
t_en: I betrayed Mr. Stefan's trust. He put so much trust into me, but I... I couldn't even do this one simple thing.

t_ch: 我要想办法补救一切。我必须补救，否则……
t_en: I'm trying to fix everything and make amends, or else...

t_ch: 你不再需要我的帮助了吗？
t_en: Do you no longer need my help?

t_ch: …不，不需要了。我们之间的交流…应该到此为止了。
t_en: ...No, no, thank you. Your involvement... should probably end here.

t_ch: 对不起，我不想冒犯…多亏了你，我才能找回这些宝贵的记忆。但这也是为了你着想……
t_en: I'm sorry. I didn't want to offend... It's thanks to you that I have all these precious memories back, but this is also for your sake...

t_ch: 如果我们交流得太多…如果我说了不该说的话，斯捷潘先生，他一定会……
t_en: If we exchange too much information... if I say something that I shouldn't... then Mr. Stefan will definitely...

t_ch: 我…没什么想说的了。
t_en: I... don't have anything to say.

t_ch: 对不起…我知道你为了帮助我付出了很多时间。你值得一个答案……
t_en: I'm sorry... I know you put a lot of time into helping me, and you deserve an answer...

t_ch: 但我不能和你分享这一切。我不想让你被卷到不好的事情里，我不希望看到善良的人被伤害……
t_en: But I can't just share this with you. I don't want you to get caught up in bad business like this. I don't want to see a good person get hurt...

t_ch: 所以…就到此为止吧。我会记住你的善意，但请你努力忘记这一切…忘记我。
t_en: So... let's stop there. I'll remember your kindness, but please try to forget all about this... all about me.

t_ch: （希莉儿让我忘记发生的事，还说不希望我受到伤害……）
t_en: (Cyrille wants me to forget everything, and says she doesn't want me to get hurt...)

t_ch: （虽然桑博说这可能是裂界影响下产生的幻象…但她的语气听上去实在太过真实了。）
t_en: (I know Sampo said it's possibly all just an illusion created by the Fragmentum... but she sounds all too real to me.)

t_ch: （无论如何，还是把这件事报告给负责禁区治安的人吧…）
t_en: (In any case, let's report this to the security personnel responsible for the restricted zone...)

t_ch: 前线压力越来越大，现在必须得调整铁卫的战术策略了……
t_en: The pressure on the front lines is increasing, and now the strategy of the Silvermane Guards must be adjusted...

t_ch: 外来者，来聊聊吧——你现在方便吗？
t_en: Outsider, let's talk. Are you free right now?

t_ch: 我在禁区里遇到了一个奇怪的女人…
t_en: I met this strange woman in the restricted zone...

t_ch: 奇怪的女人？确定不是后勤队或者科研部的成员吗？
t_en: A strange woman? Are you sure it wasn't someone from the logistics team or Scientific Research Division?

t_ch: 嗯…给我讲讲你的发现吧。如果有必要，我会即刻派铁卫开展调查。
t_en: Mmm... Tell me what you found. If necessary, I'll immediately send some Silvermane Guards over to investigate.

t_ch: 把你遇到希莉儿的事转告给了邓恩……
t_en: You tell Dunn about your encounter with Cyrille...

t_ch: 唔…满嘴胡话的女人，凭空出现的军械箱……
t_en: Mmm... a strange woman spouting nonsense, and Armory Boxes just popping out of thin air...

t_ch: 听上去…确实有些离谱，不过想想是从你嘴里说出来的，倒也正常。
t_en: It sounds... utterly outrageous, but then again, it doesn't seem that strange coming from you.

t_ch: 那片区域是堆放淘汰军械的库房，平日里确实少有人经过——不瞒你说，我之前也听过一些古怪的传闻……
t_en: That's a warehouse stacked with old abandoned ammo, and nobody really goes by there at all — but to tell the truth, I've heard a few weird rumors in my time...

t_ch: 情况我大概了解了。我过后会派铁卫去彻底勘察一下情况，顺便把库房做一次大清理。
t_en: Anyway I think I got the gist of the situation. I'll send some Silvermane Guards over to investigate things thoroughly — and give the warehouse a once-over at the same time.

t_ch: 你认识一个叫斯捷潘的人吗？
t_en: Do you know or have you heard about a guy called Stefan?

t_ch: …斯捷潘？不认识，从来没听过这名字。你找他有什么事吗？我能帮上什么忙么？
t_en: ...Stefan? No, never heard of him. Have you got some business with him? Anything I can help out with?

t_ch: …算了，当我没说。
t_en: ...Never mind. Forget I said anything.

t_ch: 嗯？你的反应有点奇怪…嗐，我就不多问了。
t_en: Hmm? That's a bit weird of you... Eh, I won't pry.

t_ch: 能帮我查查这个名字吗？
t_en: Could you help me look into this name?

t_ch: 没问题。我这就派人去核对下铁卫名册。
t_en: No problem. I'll send someone to check the Silvermane Guard roster.

t_ch: 总之，感谢你为维护禁区秩序做出的贡献，外来者！如果发现了什么情况，我会第一时间通知你的。
t_en: Anyways, thanks for your contribution to maintaining order here in the restricted zone, outsider. I'll give you a heads-up as soon as I hear anything.

t_ch: 你说你在仓库里有些发现？
t_en: You said that you found something in the warehouse?

t_ch: 铁卫小队没在仓库里发现什么异常现象，不过倒是找到了些奇怪的书信…我们大概扫了一眼，但大伙都对里面的内容毫无头绪。
t_en: My Silvermane Guard squad never found anything strange in the warehouse other than a few weird letters... We scanned them over but nobody had any idea about what they said.

t_ch: 我在想，说不定你能从中发现些什么…不如就把这些东西交给你吧。
t_en: I think you might be able to get something out of them... Why don't I leave these with you?

t_ch: 哦，对了——我去查了下「斯捷潘」这个名字，但在部队的名册里没有相关记录，也包括已经处于退役状态的铁卫。
t_en: Ah yes — I checked up on that "Stefan" character you mentioned. There's no record of him among our registers, including retired Silvermane Guards and veterans.

t_ch: 看来，这件事我是帮不上什么忙了。如果你还打算做后续调查的话，祝你好运！
t_en: Looks like I won't be able to help you out. If you want to conduct your own follow-up investigation, I wish you all the best!

t_ch: 谢谢你的善意，陌生人。很抱歉我不能对你道出真相……
t_en: Thank you for your kindness, stranger. I'm sorry that I can't tell you everything...

t_ch: 请原谅我，这是为了你的安全着想…请你忘记我吧。
t_en: Please forgive me. It's for your own safety... Please, just forget about me.

t_ch: 这就是…杰帕德提到的那片裂界？
t_en: Is this... the Fragmentum Gepard was talking about?

t_ch: 边缘通路受到的侵蚀跟这里比起来，完全是小巫见大巫。
t_en: Compared to this, the corrosion we saw in Backwater Pass pales into insignificance.

t_ch: 怎么定位「星核」的位置？
t_en: How can we pinpoint the Stellaron?

t_ch: 希露瓦说过，星核很可能被藏在贝洛伯格北部的雪原中。想抵达那里，恐怕得先找到这座裂界迷宫的出口。
t_en: Serval said that the Stellaron was very likely hidden in the Snow Plains north of Belobog. If we want to get there, I'm afraid we'll have to find the exit to this Fragmentum maze first.

t_ch: 我说…你们有没有觉得这地方很诡异？感觉就像…感觉就像我们在被好多双眼睛一直盯着。
t_en: Guys... does this place feel weird to you? It feels like... like there are loads of eyes staring at us.

t_ch: 这里的氛围让人不安。
t_en: There's a restless atmosphere here.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}说的没错，感觉就像…感觉就像我们在被好多双眼睛一直盯着。
t_en: Oh yeah, {NICKNAME}. It feels like... like there are loads of eyes staring at us.

t_ch: 希露瓦说过，星核很可能被藏在贝洛伯格北部的雪原中。想抵达那里，恐怕得先找到这座裂界迷宫的出口。
t_en: Serval said that the Stellaron was very likely hidden in the Snow Plains north of Belobog. If we want to get there, I'm afraid we'll have to find the exit to this Fragmentum maze first.

t_ch: 这地方…让人起鸡皮疙瘩，不舒服。皮肤下面好像有虫子在爬。
t_en: ...This place makes my flesh crawl... like there are insects under my skin.

t_ch: 现在我们没有向导，也没有能直接指向星核的探测手段——只能步步为营。
t_en: We have no guide and no way of detecting the Stellaron. We'll just have to advance gradually... and cautiously.

t_ch: 但也不必悲观。裂界的污染越重，就证明我们离根源越近了。
t_en: Don't be disheartened. The stronger the Fragmentum contamination, the closer we are to the source.

t_ch: 看，前面的地上…好像有什么东西？
t_en: Look, up ahead on the ground... What is that?

t_ch: 多长个心眼，不要随便碰裂界里的东西。
t_en: Be careful, think twice before you interact with anything in the Fragmentum.

t_ch: 这是——布洛妮娅的！
t_en: This is... Bronya's!

t_ch: 是在孤儿院找到的…
t_en: We found it in the orphanage...

t_ch: 对！难道…难道她已经来过这里了？
t_en: Yeah! So she's... she's been here already?

t_ch: 难道她也在这里？
t_en: Could she be here too?

t_ch: 对，绝对没错！否则不会有那么巧的事……
t_en: Right, she must be! This can't be a coincidence.

t_ch: 呜、呜哇！你们快看！
t_en: Wh—Whoa! Quick, look!

t_ch: 那是布洛妮娅！欸、欸！怎么还有可可利亚？！
t_en: That's Bronya! H—Huh? Is that Cocolia too!?

t_ch: 不，那不是她们。
t_en: No, it isn't them.

t_ch: 那里的是…某种残影？是裂界复制出来的某种能量残留……
t_en: It must be... a kind of echo? Some type of residual energy replicated by the Fragmentum...

t_ch: 那就是说她们肯定来过这儿了，对吧？
t_en: Which means they were definitely here, right?

t_ch: 而且看样子，她们是往那个方向去了。
t_en: And it looks like they went that way.

t_ch: 哈，正好！咱们还正愁找不到向导呢。走吧！
t_en: Ha, just in time! We had no idea which direction to take. Let's go!

t_ch: 被裂界侵蚀的物件可能不稳定。换做是我，不会去碰那个装置。
t_en: Objects that have been exposed to Fragmentum corrosion might not be stable. I wouldn't touch that device if I were you.

t_ch: …你还没告诉我这究竟是什么地方，母亲。
t_en: ...You still haven't told me what this place is, mother.

t_ch: 七百年前，这里曾是贝洛伯格的北方边界，也是阿丽萨•兰德带领第一批银鬃铁卫抵御军团的古战场。
t_en: Seven hundred years ago, this was Belobog's northern border and the site of an ancient battle. Here is where Alisa Rand led the first Silvermane Guards in resistance against the Legion.

t_ch: 至于现在…只是一座被废弃的回廊，处处回荡着旧世界的残响。
t_en: Now... it is nothing more than an abandoned corridor, filled with the sounds of Old World echoes.

t_ch: 但不要被这支离破碎的表象迷惑，布洛妮娅。等到承诺兑现的那一天…这片废墟将成为新世界的温床。
t_en: Don't let its fragmented appearance fool you, Bronya. On the day the promise is fulfilled... these ruins will become the breeding ground of a new world.

t_ch: …母亲，你真的相信「星核」作出的承诺吗？它唤来了墙外的风雪，开启了裂界的大门。它毁灭了我们的文明——
t_en: ...Mother, do you truly believe the Stellaron's promise? It summoned the blizzard beyond our walls and opened the door of the Fragmentum. It destroyed our civilization, it...

t_ch: ——而我们的先祖犯下的过错，就是没能尽早拥抱毁灭。
t_en: ...And yet, the crime of our ancestors was their inability to embrace that destruction sooner...

t_ch: 自天外的入侵者降临那一刻起，这个世界就已经被宣判死刑。但星核…它给我们保留了一丝希望。
t_en: From the moment those intruders descended from beyond the sky, this world was handed a death sentence. But the Stellaron... has reserved for us a thread of hope.

t_ch: 重获新生需要付出代价。抹除一切属于旧世界的陈腐印迹，放弃毫无意义的苟延残喘…这就是「星核」开出的价码。
t_en: There is a price to pay for new life. Wiping away every decaying vestige of the Old World, renouncing the meaningless and lingering struggle... This is the price the Stellaron demands.

t_ch: 祂的承诺兑现后，布洛妮娅…我们就不必再穷尽此生守护一片废土。你和我，我们会亲眼见证新世界从废墟中升起。
t_en: When the promise is fulfilled, Bronya... no longer will we have to squander our lives guarding this wasteland. You and I will witness the new world rise from the ruins.

t_ch: 这机关好复杂啊，她们是怎么过去的？
t_en: This is a complex mechanism. How did they get past?

t_ch: 唉，咱就知道事情没那么简单……
t_en: *sigh* I knew this wouldn't be simple...

t_ch: 对了！刚才那片空地上，是不是有个像「能源中枢」的大家伙？回去看看吧，说不定跟这扇大门有什么联系呢？
t_en: Wait! Wasn't there some big energy hub-looking thing back in that empty area? Let's go back and take a look, maybe it's linked to this gate.

t_ch: 我不赞成与深层裂界里的物件做过多接触，但是……
t_en: I can't say I like the idea of interacting with our surroundings — especially this deep into the Fragmentum. However...

t_ch: 但是？
t_en: However?

t_ch: …别无他法，唯有置之死地而后生。
t_en: ...There's no other way. Desperate times call for desperate measures.

t_ch: 嘿，理智如丹恒，也有向冒险精神妥协的一天嘛！
t_en: Ha, even Dan Heng the sensible has his adventurous days!

t_ch: 机关有反应了？
t_en: It worked, right?

t_ch: …不过大门还是纹丝不动呢……
t_en: ...But, the gate didn't move an inch...

t_ch: 是不是还有其它的装置？
t_en: *sigh* Are there other units around?

t_ch: 嗯。很明显，机关的设计者不希望外人轻易通过。接着寻找能源中枢吧。
t_en: Hmm. It's clear that whoever designed these mechanisms didn't want outsiders to get through too easily. Let's keep looking for the energy hub.

t_ch: 看那边！我好像看到下一个装置了。
t_en: Look over there! I think I can see the next unit.

t_ch: 我也看到了，走吧。
t_en: ...I see it too — let's go.

t_ch: 那些怪物，好像在把守能源中枢？
t_en: Are those monsters guarding the energy hub?

t_ch: 似乎如此…奇怪，裂界怪物应该会破坏人造物件才对。
t_en: It seems like it... Strange. Fragmentum monsters would usually destroy man-made objects.

t_ch: 你们听到声音了吗？这道机关肯定也打开啦！
t_en: Did you hear that? This mechanism must be on now!

t_ch: 走吧，去找下一个！
t_en: Come on, let's find the next one!

t_ch: 我从来没见过这么荒凉的地方…至少铆钉镇上还留存着矿民的生活气息。
t_en: I've never seen such a desolate place... At least in Rivet Town, you could tell that people used to live there.

t_ch: 铆钉镇不过是裂界伸出的一根触角——而我们现在正深入心脏。
t_en: Rivet Town is only at the outstretched fingertips of the corrosion... Now, we're deep in the Fragmentum's heart.

t_ch: 成功！这道机关也关闭了。
t_en: Success! Another mechanism shut down.

t_ch: 下一个机关…啊，就在那扇门后面。
t_en: Where is the next mechanism... Oh, behind that gate.

t_ch: 没有开关，想办法绕路过去吧。
t_en: There's no switch. We need to find a way around.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我有个问题想问你。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I want to ask you a question.

t_ch: 你问吧。
t_en: Ask away.

t_ch: 非要在这个时候问吗？
t_en: Is now a good time?

t_ch: 嗯，我认为这就是最合适的时机。
t_en: Yes, I think this could well be the best time.

t_ch: 你之前提到过的那些梦境，还记得吗？
t_en: Do you still remember those dreams you mentioned to us?

t_ch: 我知道你很清醒，但我需要你尝试一下，看看能否复现梦里的「声音」。
t_en: I know you're wide awake right now, but I need you to try. See if you can recall the "voice" you heard.

t_ch: （闭上眼睛冥思）
t_en: (Close eyes and meditate)

t_ch: 你试图回忆那个怪梦，随着思绪的深入，那声音也适时闯入你的脑海……
t_en: You try to recall the dreams. As your train of thought deepens, the voice comes to you...

t_ch: （试着敷衍了事）
t_en: (Give it a halfhearted attempt)

t_ch: 你闭上眼睛，对回忆并无指望。但那声音却适时闯入你的脑海……
t_en: You close your eyes but don't expect to remember anything. And yet, the voice comes to you...

t_ch: 可可…利亚…威胁…渐进……
t_en: Danger... approaches...

t_ch: 必须…根除…必须…彻底……
t_en: You must...eradicate... Once... and for all...

t_ch: 如何？
t_en: Well?

t_ch: …有个声音在呼唤可可利亚…
t_en: There was... a voice calling out to Cocolia...

t_ch: 那声音好像知道我们在接近。
t_en: The voice... it knows we're getting close.

t_ch: （尝试复述方才听见的低语）
t_en: (Try to repeat the whispers you heard)

t_ch: 以下只是我的推测——你听到的声音，或许是星核以某种方式向可可利亚发出的讯息。
t_en: This is merely speculation... but perhaps the voice you heard is the Stellaron somehow conveying information to Cocolia...

t_ch: 而你之所以能听见，是因为你身体里的东西…和这个世界的「星核」产生了共鸣。
t_en: ...and the one in your body... is resonating with the Stellaron on this world.

t_ch: 啊？！你的意思是「星核」之间能隔空传话？这我可从来没听说过……
t_en: Huh!? You mean that Stellarons can communicate? I've never heard that before...

t_ch: 之前也没有人类能在体内容纳一颗「星核」。
t_en: Well, there's never been a human that could accommodate a Stellaron in their body.

t_ch: 「星核」已经警告了可可利亚…
t_en: The Stellaron has already warned Cocolia...

t_ch: …那，我们岂不是主动往圈套里跳？
t_en: ...Then, aren't we walking straight into a trap?

t_ch: 是。而布洛妮娅很可能就是诱饵。
t_en: ...Yes. And Bronya may well be the bait.

t_ch: 既然是丹恒的猜想，十有八九没跑儿啦。但就算我们提前猜到了，现在也没办法打道回府啊。
t_en: Dan Heng's speculations are usually right on the money. But even if we've guessed right, we can't go home now.

t_ch: 哼——反正都没有退路了，至少我们做好了准备，不会被可可利亚唬住。有埋伏又怎样？！我们才不会输哩！
t_en: Hmph... there's no way back anyway. At least Cocolia won't catch us off guard now. So what if it's an ambush!? We're not about to lose now!

t_ch: 我要救下布洛妮娅，打爆可可利亚，再把那什么星核砍得粉碎！
t_en: I have to save Bronya, beat Cocolia, and crush this Stellaron thing to dust!

t_ch: 「星核」知道我们来了…
t_en: The Stellaron knows we're here...

t_ch: 成功啦！这下机关门应该就被破解了，可真是费了不少功夫啊……
t_en: Done! It took some effort, but we finally cracked these mechanisms...

t_ch: 离追上布洛妮娅又进了一步。快走吧！
t_en: We're one step closer to Bronya. Let's move!

t_ch: 咝！这也是残影吗？
t_en: *gasp* Are these echoes too?

t_ch: 没错。这里的裂界似乎与可可利亚产生了特殊的联系，它正在反复重现她的形象。
t_en: Yes. The Fragmentum here seems to have made a special connection with Cocolia. It's repeatedly generating forms in her image.

t_ch: 我不明白…假如真相如你所说，可是过去的几百年里，在位的每一任守护者都在抗拒星核的召唤……
t_en: I don't understand... Even if the truth is as you say, every generation of guardian has resisted the call of the Stellaron...

t_ch: 难道她们都做错了吗，母亲？
t_en: Can all of them have been wrong, mother?

t_ch: 你还没看清吗，布洛妮娅？这里没有对错，有的只是人类的傲慢，还有短视。
t_en: Do you still not see, Bronya? There is no right or wrong here. Some of them acted out of human pride, others out of human shortsightedness.

t_ch: 我们的先祖——包括阿丽萨•兰德在内——他们为了延续这一方文明燃尽生命，然后归于沉寂，记忆被风雪吹散。
t_en: Our ancestors... including Alisa Rand... spent their whole lives trying to prolong this civilization. Then, after all fell still, their memories were scattered by the blizzard.

t_ch: 他们毕生忙于编撰颂扬人类勇气的赞歌，却抽不出时间凝神仰望星空。
t_en: They spent lifetimes writing songs of praise that lauded the bravery of humanity, but couldn't spare the time to gaze up into the stars.

t_ch: 于星空中更加宏伟的存在而言，千年的光阴不过一粟，渺小种族的成就毫无注目的价值。
t_en: To those entities of greater magnificence in the heavens above, a thousand years is but a passing moment, the attainments of insignificant beings nothing more than a footnote.

t_ch: 我不会重蹈她们的覆辙，走在绝路上顾影自怜。如果坚持注定徒劳，那就选择崭新的开始。
t_en: I shall not follow the guardians' misguided steps down that blind alley of narcissism. If persistence is futile, then one must choose a new beginning.

t_ch: 但是，「存护」的克里珀——祂难道不是你所说的「宏伟存在」之一吗？一直在庇护着贝洛伯格的…不正是祂的力量吗？
t_en: But, Qlipoth the Preservation... Are THEY not one of the magnificent entities you speak of? Is it not THEIR strength... that has sheltered Belobog this entire time?

t_ch: 「存护」？「存护」何曾正视过人类一眼？那不过是筑城者自以为是的妄想而已。
t_en: The Preservation... Has the Preservation ever looked humanity in the eye? Nothing more than an arrogant delusion of the Architects.

t_ch: 你会听到真正的宏伟之声，布洛妮娅…然后，你会理解我的选择。
t_en: You will hear the real voice of magnificence, Bronya... Then, you will understand my choice.

t_ch: 别有洞天呀这地方！瞧那些桌子和岗哨，以前有铁卫驻扎在这里吗？
t_en: Whoa, check this place out! Tables and sentry posts — there must have been Silvermane Guards stationed here once.

t_ch: 就算有也荒废很久了。或许这儿藏着什么有价值的信息…可惜我们没时间四处翻腾。
t_en: If there were, they left a long time ago. There might be valuable information here... shame we don't have time to look for it.

t_ch: 又是从前没见过的新形态怪物呢。
t_en: Looks like another completely new monster.

t_ch: 考虑到我们的所在之处，不冒出些新奇的敌人才是怪事。
t_en: Considering where we are, it would be surprising if we didn't run into strange enemies here.

t_ch: 管你是什么鬼怪离奇，别想挡我们的路——上吧！
t_en: I don't care how strange it is, it's blocking our way... Let's get it!

t_ch: 这个敌人很不一样呢！要不是知道它是裂界的产物，还以为是在和人类战斗哩。
t_en: This enemy is completely different! If I didn't know it was a product of the Fragmentum, I'd think we were fighting a human!

t_ch: 和胡乱破坏的低级裂界生物不同，它好像有自己的思想，甚至会使用高明的战术。
t_en: Unlike those random low-level Fragmentum creations, this one seems to have a mind of its own. It seems to be able to use complex tactics.

t_ch: 看，布洛妮娅和可可利亚的残影——她们肯定也经过了这里。
t_en: Look, Bronya and Cocolia's echoes... they must have come through here.

t_ch: 声音变得清晰了…
t_en: The voice is getting clearer...

t_ch: 咦？那岂不是说明我们已经很接近了？
t_en: ...That means we must be really close, right?

t_ch: 意识变得模糊了…
t_en: I'm starting to feel hazy...

t_ch: 喂，坚持住！可就差这临门一脚了呀！
t_en: Hey, stay with it! This is the final push!

t_ch: 我有预感，我们离北方的雪原已经很近了。
t_en: I sense that we're very near to the northern Snow Plains.

t_ch: 第一任守护者在这里指挥银鬃铁卫作战，用巨大的牺牲换来了短暂的喘息之机。
t_en: This is where the first guardian led the Silvermane Guards into battle. She made huge sacrifices for the sake of temporary respite.

t_ch: 但来自异世界的军团不知疲倦。她很快就意识到，血肉之躯根本不可能与如此可怕的敌人抗衡。
t_en: But the otherworldly Legion did not tire. She soon realized that flesh and blood could not contend with so ruthless an enemy.

t_ch: 几近绝望之际…她选择把目光投向「星核」，许下最初的愿望。
t_en: When all was nearly lost, she chose to set her sights on the Stellaron... and made the first wish.

t_ch: 之后发生的事…你也清楚了。
t_en: As for what came later... you know as well as I.

t_ch: 所以…「寒潮」其实是应人类的愿望产生的灾难……
t_en: So... the Eternal Freeze was a disaster brought about by a human wish...?

t_ch: 讽刺吗？星核只是实现了人类的诉愿，我们却在接下来的几百年里对它的存在讳莫如深。
t_en: Ironic, no? All the Stellaron did was answer humanity's call, and yet we kept its existence a closely guarded secret for centuries.

t_ch: 甚至还妄图用「它」来抑制星核…可悲，可笑。
t_en: Even going so far as to attempt to use "it" to control the Stellaron... Woeful... laughable.
It?

t_ch: 用不属于这个世界的技术打造出来的东西——旧时代的「公司」和筑城者妄图用来收容星核的抑制器。
t_en: Something created using otherworldly technology... An inhibitor that a corporation from the Old World and the Architects attempted to use to house the Stellaron.

t_ch: 我知道你有很多疑问，女儿。耐心些…很快你就能得到所有答案。
t_en: ...I know you have many questions, my daughter. Be patient... soon you will have all the answers.

t_ch: 又是那个女人的影子…这次布洛妮娅不在她身边。
t_en: It's that "shadow" of hers again... but Bronya's isn't with her this time.

t_ch: 反正只是裂界造出来的幻象而已，{NICKNAME}，咱们接近点看看。
t_en: It's only a Fragmentum illusion. {NICKNAME}, let's get a closer look.

t_ch: 总感觉…和之前的残影有些不太一样？
t_en: It feels like this echo is different from the others before.

t_ch: 侵入者……
t_en: Intruders...

t_ch: 欸？她…她说话了吗？
t_en: Huh!? Did... Did she just speak!?

t_ch: 不得…接近……
t_en: You cannot... approach...

t_ch: 不好——三月，让开！
t_en: Not good — March, out the way!

t_ch: …星核！
t_en: ...the Stellaron!

t_ch: 入侵者…止步于此！
t_en: Intruders... You go no further!

t_ch: 这、这家伙，真的不是本尊吗？
t_en: A—Are you sure this isn't the real her?

t_ch: 外表再像，裂界复制出来的始终是赝品。打倒她！
t_en: It may look like her, but the Fragmentum can only create forgeries. Bring her down!

t_ch: …消散了呢。
t_en: ...She disappeared.

t_ch: 那段阶梯…只有这一条路了。
t_en: Those stairs... it's the only way forward.

t_ch: 不管它通向何处，想来就是这趟旅途的终点。
t_en: Wherever they lead, it must be the end of the journey.

t_ch: 我可不想这么快就到终点啊…我希望我的旅途永远没有尽头。
t_en: I don't wanna get to the end so soon... I want my journey to go on forever.

t_ch: 也就是说…布洛妮娅，还有上下层区的命运，现在就在前方…就掌握在我们的手里。
t_en: This means that Bronya is up ahead... and so is the fate of the Overworld and the Underworld... It's all in our hands.

t_ch: 奇怪，我本以为自己会害怕，或是紧张…但是没有。我现在唯一的感觉…就是不真实。
t_en: Strange, I thought I'd feel nervous or afraid... but I don't. The only feeling I have... is that none of this seems real.

t_ch: 「开拓」之旅总会这样收尾吗？
t_en: Is this how Trailblazing expeditions usually come to an end?

t_ch: 也不会，有时候收尾挺欢乐的。
t_en: Not always, sometimes it's a happier conclusion.

t_ch: 他俩应该已经习惯这种冒险了。
t_en: These two have gotten used to these expeditions.

t_ch: 老实说，很难习惯啊！以前都得依靠姬子和杨叔……
t_en: To be honest, you never really get used to them! We always used to rely on Himeko and Mr. Yang.

t_ch: 能在雪原里安息…也挺好的。
t_en: Resting forever in the Snow Plains... that wouldn't be so bad.

t_ch: 喂，你又在说什么丧气话呀！
t_en: Hey, way to boost morale over there!

t_ch: 列车行过一个又一个世界，我等只是过客，是一段故事的见证者。
t_en: The Express has traveled world after world. We are merely passing travellers — witnesses to part of a grander story.

t_ch: 我们总是避免被卷入决定世界生死存亡的浪潮…但也总有身不由己的时候。
t_en: We invariably avoid getting drawn into turbulence that can decide the fate of a world... but there are always times when we have no choice but to act.

t_ch: 换句话说，{NICKNAME}——你的初次「开拓」之旅，运气好到爆棚啦！
t_en: Or to put it another way, {NICKNAME}... this is one heck of a first Trailblazing expedition! You hit the jackpot!

t_ch: 我本打算说些多余的灰心话，想想还是算了。跟你们搭伙冒险，总觉得再大的困难都不在话下。
t_en: I was about to bore you all with my gloomy reflections... but on second thought — not today. Adventuring with you guys by my side has made me feel like no obstacle is too big.

t_ch: 没错！愉快的冒险本来就没有那么多条条框框嘛。
t_en: Right on! But you can say whatever you like — a good adventure shouldn't be full of rules and regulations!

t_ch: 帮助需要帮助的人们，结交值得信赖的伙伴，顺手再拯救几次世界——
t_en: Helping those in need, befriending those worth counting on, and saving the world a few times along the way...

t_ch: ——这都是「开拓的精神」！
t_en: This is the spirit of trailblazing!

t_ch: 裂界的深处…不知道会有什么潜藏的危险呢。
t_en: We're deep inside the Fragmentum... There could be hidden dangers lying in wait.

t_ch: 还是先找找看别的线索吧？
t_en: Let's look for other clues first.

t_ch: 当着敌人的面操作也太危险了——先把这片区域清理干净再说！
t_en: Hey, it's too dangerous to operate the mechanism with them here... We better clean this place up first!

t_ch: 唔…好像不太对？
t_en: Uh... Is this right?

t_ch: 想到对岸去的话，恐怕得再旋转一次栈桥，然后绕路过去。
t_en: If we want to get to the other side, we'll need to rotate the gear bridge again and go around.

t_ch: 放任敌人不管的话，我们有可能遭到前后夹攻。
t_en: If we avoid the enemy, we might be ambushed further in.

t_ch: 到时候后悔就来不及了——就在这里击败它吧。
t_en: It'll be too late for regrets then... Let's defeat this one here.

t_ch: …好…好冷！光凭一点点开拓的力量，已经没法抵御低温了……
t_en: It's f—freezing here — the blizzard's so fierce! The power from the Trailblaze Path isn't enough to stop the cold...

t_ch: 这说明我们正在靠近星核…靠近寒潮的中心。
t_en: It means that we're getting closer to the Stellaron... and the heart of the Eternal Freeze.

t_ch: 这是…「筑城者」留下的遗迹吗？
t_en: Did the Architects build this...?

t_ch: …跟城里那些一板一眼的建筑完全不同。
t_en: ...very different from the uniform style of the buildings we saw in the city.

t_ch: 看起来…像不像一只巨大的手掌？
t_en: Does it... look like the palm of a huge hand to you?

t_ch: 这是什么东西啊…噫，真可怕！
t_en: Wh—What is this? ...It's scary!

t_ch: 就像被琥珀困住的昆虫……
t_en: ...It's like an insect trapped in amber...

t_ch: 反物质军团的爪牙入侵这个世界时，一定没想到自己会被寒潮吞没吧。
t_en: I bet the Antimatter Legion forces never thought they'd be swallowed up by the Eternal Freeze when they invaded this world.

t_ch: 要不给他解冻试试？
t_en: Wanna defrost them and find out?

t_ch: 不了不了，解冻了也是添堵。
t_en: No, no! We've got enough on our plate.

t_ch: 你会不会也是这样…
t_en: Is this how you were...?

t_ch: 我…还是记不起来啊…没有印象……
t_en: I... I don't remember... It's a blank canvas...

t_ch: 给他起名七月三好了。
t_en: I hereby name him: March 8th.

t_ch: 亏你这时候还有心情开玩笑啊……
t_en: I'm impressed you can still think up jokes right now...

t_ch: 不过，冰块里这些家伙还活着吗……
t_en: Even in the ice... could these guys still be alive...?

t_ch: 商业区沦陷了。居民已经转移，暂时安置在外城区。戍卫队…牺牲二十六人，但我……
t_en: The Commercial District has fallen. We've transfered the residents to the Outer Municipal District for the time being. The Garrison lost <unbreak>26</unbreak> fighters... but I...

t_ch: …说下去，杰帕德。
t_en: ...Go on, Gepard.

t_ch: 我…我不确定牺牲的铁卫是不是真的死去了。我看到他们残破的身形在裂界中徘徊……
t_en: I... I'm not sure that they're dead... I saw their broken figures lingering in the Fragmentum...

t_ch: 那里发生的事违背常理，接近疯狂。守护者大人，情况如果持续下去……
t_en: What happened back there defied all reason... it was madness. Madam Guardian, if this continues...

t_ch: …我很清楚后果，杰帕德。退下吧。
t_en: ...I know well the consequences, Gepard. You may leave.

t_ch: …我们很清楚后果。
t_en: ...We know well the consequences.

t_ch: ——你还在犹豫什么，可可利亚？这不是你一直在期待的吗？离开这个笼子，亲眼看看这座城市吧！我们还有什么可失去的吗？
t_en: ...Why do you hesitate, Cocolia? Isn't this what you've always been hoping for? Get out of this cage and take a look at the city. What is there to lose!?

t_ch: 前人的努力…他们未竟的研究…终于能在这个时代结出果实了！给我一支卫队，让我去找到「星核」，找到拯救家园的办法——
t_en: The efforts of our predecessors... their unfinished research... we can finally see them come to fruition! Give me some soldiers and I'll locate the Stellaron and find out how to save our world—

t_ch: …冷静下来，希露瓦。
t_en: ...You need to come to your senses, Serval.

t_ch: …冷静？你在说什——
t_en: Come... to my senses? What are you—

t_ch: 铁卫！朗道女士累了，带她下去吧。
t_en: Guards! Miss Serval is tired, take her to rest.

t_ch: 查封她的实验室，扣押所有研究人员——他们进行的是非法的研究。
t_en: Sequester her lab and detain the researchers — their work is illegal.

t_ch: …为什么，可可利亚…你为什么会变得这么冷漠……
t_en: ...Why Cocolia... Why have you grown so cold...

t_ch: 希露瓦•朗道…你曾是我最珍视的朋友。
t_en: Serval Landau... you were my most cherished friend.

t_ch: …但约定的新世界里没有她的位置。
t_en: ...But there is no place for her in the promised new world.

t_ch: 可可利亚大人，怪物正向这边涌来！这里…这里太危险了！
t_en: Madam Cocolia, the monsters are surging this way! ...It's too dangerous here!

t_ch: …愚蠢，卑微……
t_en: ...Foolish, weak...

t_ch: …什么？大人，您在说什么？
t_en: ...What? Madam, what are you saying?

t_ch: 没人能理解…除了我。他们耳目健全，却看不见、听不到……
t_en: Only I... Only I can understand. They have eyes that do not see, ears that do not hear...

t_ch: 呜哇啊啊啊啊——
t_en: ...Aghhhhhhhh...!

t_ch: 把愚昧的意志当作食粮…赦免他们的愚蠢和恐惧……
t_en: Feast on their willful ignorance... absolve them of their stupidity and fear...

t_ch: 母亲大人，我还是不明白…
t_en: Mother... I still don't understand.

t_ch: 裂界侵蚀已经蔓延到了下层。如果撤走所有铁卫…留在下面的人该怎么保护自己？
t_en: The Fragmentum corrosion has already spread to the Underworld. How will those below ground fend for themselves if we withdraw every guard?

t_ch: 自动机兵防线会负责他们的安全。最重要的防线在上层区，我们一旦崩溃，地下将瞬间覆灭。必须不惜一切代价维持防线。
t_en: The automatons will ensure their safety. The most important defensive position is here on the surface. If our lines crumble, the underground will vanish in an instant. Our defenses here must be maintained at all costs.

t_ch: 这是取舍，布洛妮娅——身为领袖，就一定要有决断的胆识……
t_en: These are the choices we make, Bronya... As a leader, you must harbor the courage and foresight to choose.

t_ch: 你、我，我们还需要履行更重要的使命…早晚有一天，你也要做出抉择
t_en: You, me, and every Silvermane Guard in the city, all of us must carry out a higher mission. There will come a day when you must make your own choice. Do you understand?

t_ch: 更重要的使命…？
t_en: Higher mission...?

t_ch: 为时尚早，布洛妮娅…为时尚早。总有一天你会明白的。
t_en: It is too early Bronya... still too early. You will understand when the time is right.

t_ch: …不…不要……
不要抗拒，布洛妮娅——接受共同的意志吧！
t_en: Do not resist, Bronya... Accept the common will!

t_ch: 不…这…不是我想要的……
t_en: No... I... I don't want this...

t_ch: 看着他们承诺的未来，布洛妮娅！一个没有贫穷、寒冷、痛苦的世界；一个人们不必再像囚徒般祈求存续的世界；一个我们可以永远守护的世界。
t_en: Look upon their promised future, Bronya! A world without poverty, without cold, without suffering... A world where people no longer have to pray like prisoners for survival... A world that we can guard for all eternity.

t_ch: 七百年来，我们不断尝试，不断抗争，以为人性的光芒将指引我们走向复兴。结果呢？我们一败涂地！
t_en: Seven hundred years ago, we tried and fought unceasingly, believing that the radiance of human nature could shepherd us towards rejuvenation. And to what end? A crushing defeat!

t_ch: 为何在面对不可抗辩的力量时，我们想到的第一件事永远是抗拒、堵上耳朵，而非聆听它的诉求？
t_en: Why, when faced with irrefutable strength, is our first thought always to resist, to cover our ears, instead of hearing the call?

t_ch: 那就是盘踞在人性深处的，难以磨灭的愚妄和怯懦。抛弃它们，解开束缚你的枷锁！星核将带人类走向进化，祂将……
t_en: It is the conceit and cowardice entrenched in the depths of human nature — so difficult to efface. Cast them aside, break free of the chains that bind you! The Stellaron will lead humanity to evolve, and THEY will...

t_ch: 洗脑就到此为止吧，魔女！
t_en: Your brainwashing ends here, you witch!

t_ch: …希儿？
t_en: ...Seele?

t_ch: …还是来了啊。本以为这暴风雪能将你们埋葬……
t_en: ...You came. I thought the blizzards had entombed you...

t_ch: 想得美！还没打倒你以前，我们是不会倒下的！
t_en: You wish! We're not going down before you do!

t_ch: 布洛妮娅！我不清楚你们之间到底发生了什么。就算你现场解释，我大概也听不懂那些玄乎的东西。但有两件事我清楚得很——
t_en: Bronya! I don't know what happened between you and them. Even if you explained it to me, I probably wouldn't understand all the crazy details. But there are two things I do know...

t_ch: 第一，这些家伙费尽千辛万苦来到这里，为的就是封印那个叫「星核」的玩意。
t_en: One: These guys have come through hell to get here — to seal this Stellaron thing.

t_ch: 第二，记得我说过的话吗？如果你出了什么事，我一定会来救你的。
t_en: And two: Do you remember what I said? If anything happened to you, I'd save you.

t_ch: 所以，你懂吗？就算你已经被那边的女人洗脑，把我们之间的约定忘得干干净净了——我也要把你打晕了再带回去！
t_en: Do you understand? Even if the damage is done — even if you've completely forgotten our promise, then... then I'm just gonna have to knock you out and bring you back myself!

t_ch: 我们需要你——
t_en: We need you...

t_ch: ——贝洛伯格需要你，布洛妮娅。
t_en: ...Belobog needs you, Bronya.

t_ch: 希儿，还有大家……
t_en: Seele, all of you...

t_ch: 可以了吧？我已经给了你们充分的时间…道别的时间。
t_en: ...Finished? I think I've given you long enough, to... bid farewell.

t_ch: 带你来这的另一个原因，是时候告诉你了，布洛妮娅。
t_en: It's time I told you my other reason for bringing you here, Bronya.

t_ch: 我想见证你的选择。
t_en: I want to witness your choice.

t_ch: 所有真相，我都告诉你了；有关与星核的交易，有关我向它许下的愿望。我们之间…再没有秘密了。
t_en: I have told you all the truths there are to tell: The deal with the Stellaron, the wish I made to it... There are no more secrets between us.

t_ch: 许多年前，「星核」的声音第一次在我耳边响起，而我和历代守护者一样，闭目塞听。那时的我就是现在的你，苦苦守着筑城者所谓的「存护」……
t_en: Many years ago, the voice of the Stellaron sounded in my ears for the first time. I was no different from the previous generations of guardians... I withdrew, refused to hear. I was as you are now — bitterly defending the Architects' so-called "Preservation..."

t_ch: 我的信念一度无比坚定…直到一个突如其来的变量打乱了一切；另一个选择出现在我面前，那意味着颠覆旧秩序，迎接新世界的到来。
t_en: My conviction was once steadfast, unparalleled... until a sudden change arrived and threw everything into chaos. Another choice appeared before me — a subversion of the old order... and the welcoming of a new world.

t_ch: 但比起虚无缥缈，越来越遥远的「存护」，它是那么真实……
t_en: And compared to the illusory, evermore distant Preservation, this was so tangible...

t_ch: 我一直在苦想，苦想该怎么向你传达这一切。承诺中的明天总会到来，但如果你不能待在我的身旁，和我一同守望那个新世界……
t_en: I have agonized — long agonized over how to convey all this to you. Inevitably, the promised tomorrow will transpire... but if you were not there beside me, to watch over that new world...

t_ch: 那我将陷入痛苦之中——布洛妮娅，难以抹消的痛苦！
t_en: ...Then I would descend into torment, Bronya — enduring torment!

t_ch: 也许，我还要感谢你们，外来者。迫于你们带来的压力…我终于得以直面自己最后的弱点。
t_en: Perhaps I should be thanking you, outsiders. The pressure you have imposed... has at long last compelled me to confront my final... weakness.

t_ch: 布洛妮娅，从小到大，我从未逼迫你服从我的意志。无论如何，你总有选择——过去如此，如今依然。
t_en: Bronya, throughout your life, I have never forced you to submit to my will. You have always, will always have a choice... as then, so now.

t_ch: 选择吧，女儿。
t_en: Choose, my daughter.
